name: Arsenal
description_short: Arsenal Credit Union is a St. Louis-based member-owned financial institution offering checking, savings, loans, and investment services to individuals and businesses.
description_long:
Arsenal Credit Union is a not-for-profit, member-owned credit union headquartered in the St. Louis area with multiple branch locations including Arnold, Webster Groves, Florissant, and Swansea. As a federally insured credit union (NCUA), it operates on the principle of serving its members' financial needs rather than generating profits for shareholders. The credit union has been established as a community-focused financial institution with active involvement in local causes and nonprofit support through its Community Impact Fund program.

Arsenal offers a comprehensive suite of financial products spanning personal and business banking. Personal services include free and premier checking accounts (with the Premier Checking earning 4.25% APY on balances up to $15,000), savings accounts, youth savings, debit cards, and multiple loan types including auto loans, auto refinances, recreational vehicle loans, home equity lines of credit (HELOCs), personal loans, and credit cards. For business members, Arsenal provides business checking, business loans, business credit cards, and business online banking. Additional offerings include an Arsenal Investment Center, IRAs, deposit coverage information, and insurance products (auto, home, life, and AD&D) through the TruStage Insurance Program.

Arsenal distinguishes itself through its emphasis on digital accessibility and member convenience. The credit union highlights robust online and mobile banking platforms featuring bill pay automation, Zelle integration, account security controls, and a Life Goals planning tool. Current promotional offerings include auto loan refinance rebates up to $250 and HELOC products with no closing costs. The institution also emphasizes community commitment through volunteer opportunities and its 2025 Community Impact Fund, demonstrating member-first values typical of credit union operations.

As a credit union, Arsenal's primary strength lies in its member-ownership structure and local community focus. However, the website does not provide specific information about membership eligibility requirements, fee structures, loan terms, APRs for various products, or competitive rate comparisons. The physical branch network appears limited to four locations in the St. Louis metropolitan area, which may restrict accessibility for members outside this region despite digital banking options.
services: Free Checking and Premier Checking accounts with variable APY
Savings accounts and Youth Savings accounts
Auto loans and auto refinancing with promotional rebates
Home Equity Lines of Credit (HELOC) with no closing costs
Business checking, business loans, and business credit cards
IRAs and investment services through Arsenal Investment Center
Online and mobile banking with bill pay automation and Zelle
Insurance products including auto, home, life, and AD&D through TruStage
Deposit coverage and debt protection programs
pros: Premier Checking earns 4.25% APY on balances up to $15,000 with lower balance requirements than competitors
Auto loan refinance promotion offers up to $250 rebate for new, used, or refinanced auto loans
HELOC products feature zero closing costs, saving borrowers $500-$1,500
Comprehensive digital banking suite including mobile app, online banking, bill pay automation, and Zelle integration
Member-owned credit union structure means profits are returned to members rather than external shareholders
Active community engagement through Community Impact Fund providing grants to local nonprofits
Multiple loan products tailored to specific needs (auto, recreational, home equity, personal, business)
cons: Limited branch network with only four physical locations in St. Louis area, restricting in-person service access
Website does not clearly disclose membership eligibility requirements or application process
No specific APR or loan term information displayed on website for most loan products
Limited detail on business loan options, eligibility criteria, or specific terms
No pricing transparency for services, overdraft fees, or account maintenance costs
best_for: St. Louis area residents seeking community-focused banking with local branch access
Members wanting high-yield checking with Premier Checking's 4.25% APY on moderate balances
Auto buyers or existing auto loan holders interested in refinance rebates and savings
Small business owners in the credit union's service area needing business banking and loans
diagnosis: Arsenal Credit Union is best for St. Louis metropolitan area residents who prioritize community-focused banking and want competitive yields on checking accounts, particularly through the Premier Checking product. The main caveat is that membership and service access are geographically limited to the St. Louis region, and the website lacks transparent pricing and term information for most loan products, requiring members to contact the institution directly for quotes.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.

meta_description: Arsenal — NCUA-insured credit union in Arnold, MO. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
